title: 顺水推舟
summary:
In a rented apartment at the Sheep's Pool Court in Mayfair, David Hunter and Rosaline discuss a situation involving extortion. David expresses frustration over being blackmailed, noting that the value of emeralds Rosaline sold to Greer-Trex on Bond Street for four thousand pounds has since doubled. David fears they will be "bled dry" and expresses resentment over a "secret struggle" regarding André, who they previously believed was buried in Africa. To manage the crisis, David plans to pay five thousand pounds to Mr. Inoke Yadon on Tuesday night, intending to prevent Yadon from approaching the Klopp family. David adopts a defiant attitude, telling Rosaline to follow his instructions exactly as part of his "Enoch Yate Plan."

Meanwhile, Rolly receives a letter on extravagant stationery from Beatrice Lippincott. The letter invites him to Stag to discuss matters regarding the passing of his uncle and the handling of his estate. Rolly, who has known Beatrice since childhood and recalls rumors regarding her past, decides to visit her. At the pub, Beatrice leads Rolly into a private room. She informs him that the gentleman Rolly had previously inquired about, Mr. Yates, was visited the following evening by a Mr. Hunter. Beatrice explains that she noticed a connecting door between Room 4 and Room 5, which is usually hidden by a large wardrobe, but noted that the door was slightly open during her duties.
